title: mentha aquatica var. lupulina
intro: Mentha aquatica var. lupulina, also known as hop mint or wild hop mint, is a variety of the water mint plant that is native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. It has been used for centuries in traditional medicine, where it is valued for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to its high concentration of volatile oils, particularly menthone and limonene. The health benefits of mentha aquatica var. lupulina include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and act as a natural breath freshener, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies for oral health and digestive issues. Herbal preparations of mentha aquatica var. lupulina can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of health issues, including digestive problems and oral infections.
